channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: $25 Billion, $40 Billion, $50 Billion? How Much Is US Really Spending On Iran War
item.description: The US has not added repair costs to the claimed bill for the Iran war, with the Pentagon still assessing the situation. Repair costs have not been added to the claimed bill for this war; this has 'not been reflected' in the $1.5 trillion budget FY27 proposal because the Pentagon is still 'assessing' the situation.

item.title: North Korea faces 'severe' drought: State media
item.description: North Korea is experiencing a severe drought, according to state media. North Korea faces 'severe' drought: State media.

item.title: Iran Speaker Ghalibaf's Big Warning As Oil Prices Reach Historic High
item.description: Iran Speaker Ghalibaf warned against a maritime blockade, saying it would not immediately cripple oil production. Ghalibaf ridiculed the notion that a maritime blockade would immediately cripple Iran's oil production.

item.title: Putin's "Extremely Damaging Consequences" Warning To Trump In 90-Minute Call
item.description: Putin warned of extremely damaging consequences in a 90-minute call with Trump, discussing Iran and the Persian Gulf. The presidents "paid particular attention to the situation regarding Iran and in the Persian Gulf".

item.title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Trump, Putin Speak Over Phone, Discuss Iran War
item.description: Trump and Putin discussed the Iran war in a phone call, focusing on the situation in the Persian Gulf. US Israel Iran War Ceasefire News LIVE Updates:. US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Trump, Putin Speak Over Phone, Discuss Iran War.

item.title: China Accelerates Intimidation Tactics against Taiwan
item.description: China has increased military activities near Taiwan, including sending fighter jets and bombers. This escalation is part of China's ongoing efforts to intimidate Taiwan, which China claims as its own territory.

item.title: Iran Touts New Weapon After Trump Rejects Peace Plan
item.description: Iran showcased a new weapon after US President Trump rejected a peace plan proposed by Iran. The plan included reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global oil shipments.

item.title: Iranian officials denied entry into Canada for FIFA meeting weeks before World Cup
item.description: Canadian authorities denied entry to Iranian football federation officials for a FIFA meeting. The meeting took place weeks before the World Cup, potentially complicating Iran's participation in the tournament. Iranian football federation officials have been denied access into Canada for a FIFA meeting just weeks before the start of the World Cup.

item.title: Israel seizes Global Sumud Flotilla boats 1,000km away from Gaza
item.description: Israeli forces intercepted a dozen aid boats from the Global Sumud Flotilla bound for Gaza. The boats were seized approximately 1,000km away from Gaza, disrupting an effort to deliver aid to the region.

item.title: Trump says US focused on phone negotiations with Iran rather than in Pakistan
item.description: US President Trump stated that the US is focused on phone negotiations with Iran. These talks occur as the US seeks to address Iran's actions, with Trump indicating that discussions are not taking place in Pakistan.

item.title: US singer D4vd bought tools online to dispose of girl's body, prosecutors allege
item.description: US singer D4vd is accused of buying tools online to dispose of a girl's body, prosecutors allege. His attorneys maintain his innocence and have vowed to defend him vigorously. The Tiktok star's attorneys have maintained his innocence and vowed to "vigorously defend" him.

item.title: Pro-government rally in Tehran demands end to US threats
item.description: Hundreds of Iranians rallied in Tehran to demand an end to US threats and the ongoing blockade of Iranian ports. The pro-government rally was held in response to US actions.

item.title: "Lying To American Public": Pete Hegseth Under Fire In Congress Over Iran War
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced tough questions from lawmakers about the Iran war on Wednesday. Lawmakers criticized Hegseth's handling of the conflict. US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faces tough questions from lawmakers about the Iran war on Wednesday.

item.title: Hundreds of Ultra-Orthodox Jews protest Israel’s military draft
item.description: Hundreds of Ultra-Orthodox Jews blocked a main road in Israel to protest the country's mandatory military service. The protest was part of ongoing opposition to the draft. Hundreds of Ultra-Orthodox Jews blocked a main road, as part of protests against Israel’s mandatory military service.

item.title: Rs 23,69,81,75,00,000: What US Has Lost In Iran War So Far
item.description: The United States has lost over Rs 23,69,81,75,00,000 in the Iran war so far, according to estimates. The US started carrying out strikes against Iran on February 28. The United States started carrying out strikes against Iran on February 28.

item.title: Paraguay Says It’s Not Abandoning Taiwan. China Has Other Plans.
item.description: Paraguay reaffirmed its diplomatic ties with Taiwan, resisting China's efforts to sway the South American nation. Beijing has been trying to isolate Taiwan, but Paraguay remains committed to its decades-long relationship.

item.title: US charges Sinaloa governor and other Mexican officials with drug trafficking offences
item.description: The US charged the governor of Sinaloa and nine other Mexican officials with drug trafficking offences, accusing them of aiding the Sinaloa cartel's importation of narcotics into the US. The indictment includes charges of weapons offences and kidnapping.

item.title: Trump urges Tehran to ‘give up’; Ghalibaf mocks US strategy
item.description: Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf mocked the US strategy of economic pressure, as oil prices surged and tensions continued in the Hormuz Strait. US President Trump had urged Tehran to "give up" on its nuclear program.

item.title: Balendra Shah govt defers Parliament, opts for ordinances
item.description: Nepal's Balendra Shah government decided to defer Parliament sessions and instead issue ordinances. The move allows the government to push through legislation without legislative approval. Balendra Shah govt defers Parliament, opts for ordinances.

item.title: U.S. Indictment Accuses Mexican Governor of Conspiring With Sinaloa Cartel
item.description: Rubén Rocha Moya, Sinaloa's governor, was accused of conspiring with the Sinaloa Cartel. Prosecutors accused Rubén Rocha Moya, the governor of Sinaloa state, and other Mexican officials of a yearslong conspiracy to protect the powerful cartel.
